_comment_1_: CLI-declarative form of the MLB AL East Pulse diamond.
_comment_2_: Relative outflow path is resolved against this JSON's parent directory,
_comment_3_: so the example runs from any working directory:
_comment_6_: Override the default window with env vars: PULSE_START / PULSE_END (ISO 8601, UTC).
_comment_7_: CustomCurrent nodes (hitting, pitching) are loaded from pulse_outflow.py via the
_comment_8_: class field referencing HittingDrillCurrent / PitchingDrillCurrent defined there.
_doc_limitation_: PYTHON-ONLY ORCHESTRATION: The framework JSON config loader passes incorp_params verbatim from JSON; it has no mechanism to deserialize conv_dict entries (calc/inc callables) from string representations. The all_teams and standings nodes therefore omit conv_dict here. Without conv_dict applied, division_id is never coerced on MLBAllTeam instances and the HittingDrillCurrent/PitchingDrillCurrent AL East filter produces zero teams — the CLI path produces empty output. Use the Python entry (mlb_pulse.py) for a full run that applies ALL_TEAMS_CONV and STANDINGS_CONV. This is a framework gap: watershed.json would need a conv_dict resolver that maps string sigils like '@ALL_TEAMS_CONV' to callable dicts defined in the outflow sidecar.
